description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a classic mid-20th-century design, featuring a large, convex glass screen framed by a light blue bezel. The front panel is bordered by a wooden casing with rounded edges and includes two prominent white control knobs and a small rectangular speaker grille below the screen. The sides and top are finished in a glossy, dark wood veneer, while the rear panel is metallic with ventilation holes and a protruding back section for the cathode ray tube. The left side has a vented panel, and the television stands on angled wooden legs, giving it a sturdy, retro appearance. The overall construction suggests a combination of wood, metal, glass, and plastic materials, typical of early television technology. This television would be a striking feature in an urban environment, evoking nostalgia and historical context.
description: A vintage wooden television set with a convex glass screen, blue bezel, and prominent control knobs, standing on angled legs.
description_view_0: Front view showing the convex glass screen, blue bezel, two white control knobs, and a small speaker grille.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the glossy wooden finish, angled leg, and a vented panel for cooling.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the metallic back panel with ventilation holes and a protruding section for the cathode ray tube.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the smooth wooden surface and angled leg, with no visible vents or controls.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick, light blue bezel with rounded corners, made of plastic, contrasting with the wooden frame.
attribute_control_button_layout: Two control knobs on the lower front panel, arranged in a horizontal row.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
